sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id Int32, date_string String, unix_timestamp Int32, nullable_date DateTime);
SELECT id, toDateTime(date_string) || ' is the formatted date' AS formatted_date FROM test_dates WHERE toDateTime(unix_timestamp) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, toDateTime(date_string, 'Europe/London') || ' | timestamp: ' || toString(unix_timestamp) AS combined_info FROM test_dates WHERE unix_timestamp > 0;
SELECT id, toDateTime(date_string) AS converted_date FROM test_dates WHERE converted_date > toDateTime(NULL) OR converted_date IS NULL;
CREATE TABLE test_join (id Int32, other_string String);
SELECT td.id, toDateTime(td.date_string) || ' | joined: ' || tj.other_string AS join_info FROM test_dates td LEFT JOIN test_join tj ON td.id = tj.id WHERE toDateTime(td.unix_timestamp) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT toDateTime(date_string) AS parsed_date FROM test_dates GROUP BY parsed_date HAVING parsed_date >= toDateTime('2020-01-01 00:00:00')) AS subquery;
SELECT id, toDateTime(unix_timestamp) AS converted_ts, toDateTime(NULL) || ' is NULL' AS null_check FROM test_dates WHERE converted_ts > toDateTime(0);
